description_long: This is a small wooden cabin with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in reddish-brown shingles. The front facade features a single rectangular window above a protruding wooden ledge, framed by vertical wooden planks. The sides of the cabin are constructed from evenly spaced vertical wooden boards, giving it a rustic and sturdy appearance. The rear of the cabin is open, lacking a wall, which exposes the interior and suggests it may function as a shelter or open hut. The roof overhangs on all sides, providing additional protection from the elements. The overall design is simple and utilitarian, with a focus on basic shelter and natural materials.
description: A small, rustic wooden cabin with a steep gable roof, a single front window, and an open rear side. The structure is made of vertical wooden planks and features a reddish-brown shingled roof.
description_view_0: Front view showing a single rectangular window above a wooden ledge, framed by vertical planks and the overhanging gable roof.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the vertical wooden planks and the sloped roof extending beyond the wall.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the open back of the cabin, exposing the interior and the underside of the roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the vertical planks and roof overhang.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate overhangs and reddish-brown shingle texture
attribute_window_arrangement: single medium-sized rectangular window centered on the front facade
attribute_door_placement: no exterior doors visible; rear side is fully open
